body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
While working on the conservative regridder, we noticed some aspects of remap that aren't ideal. This PR fixes a few of them.

Note that all the changes outside of `src/Interfacer.jl` and `experiments/ClimaEarth/components/ocean/climaocean_helpers.jl` come from the argument reordering change.

This PR has little to no effect on performance (varies by config), but it does make the `remap!` function correctly non-allocating. So even though it doesn't necessarily improve SYPD I still think it's worth merging.

## Content
- [x] call `remap!` from `remap`, not the other way around, so that `remap!` is actually non-allocating
- [x] reduce allocations in `remap!` by setting the field's values using `field2array` , instead of allocating a new field via `array2field`
- [x] unify ordering of target/source in `remap` and `remap!` so that target consistently comes first
- [x] check CI - AMIP is identical to main, but CMIP is slightly changed (e.g. in MO length, precipitation, atmosphere density and humidity). The changes are relatively small so I think this is okay

## Performance changes
### Benchmarks
No change in performance. See [main](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-cpu-gpu-benchmarks/builds/288#_) vs [this PR](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-cpu-gpu-benchmarks/builds/287#_)

### No shared surface space
Most of our runs use `share_surface_space: true` so they exit early in the remapping functions. Runs that have this option set to false (including CMIP runs) are likely to experience the largest change in SYPD from this PR.
- CMIP with prescribed sea ice became slightly slower as of this PR ([4.943 SYPD](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7327#019af0ab-5f42-4b97-a151-d580c13ba8b3) on main, [4.845 SYPD](https://buildkite.com/clima/climacoupler-ci/builds/7334#019af179-63f9-4b58-af38-8c5368aefdec) on this PR)
- CMIP with ClimaSeaIce doesn't have a good comparison because it ran on an H100 on main, and P100 in this PR, but I would expect to see similar results as the other CMIP run.
title: Clean up remap functions; use `remap!` in `combine_surfaces!`
